summary.intent: Implement the `ars_components::overlay::drawer` agnostic core component (task #263) following the existing Dialog pattern, with spec corrections to fix core/adapter responsibility boundaries around placement resolution and snap/velocity math.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented and tested Drawer agnostic core: 54 unit tests, 26 insta snapshots, spec-conformance, proptest, doctest, llvm-cov, and mutation (176 mutants, 0 missed) all passing. Committed and pushed PR with `Closes #263`.

repo: Dialog (`crates/ars-components/src/overlay/dialog/mod.rs`) is the canonical reference implementation pattern for all new overlay agnostic cores.
Z-index uses an adapter feedback loop: core emits `AllocateZIndex` effect, adapter responds with `SetZIndex(u32)` event, attrs render `--ars-z-index`; core never stores DOM-resolved values.
Focus trapping and restoration are pure named effects (`FocusInitial`, `FocusFirstTabbable`, `RestoreFocus`); the agnostic core never queries, stores, or resolves live DOM elements.
Snap points are fractions in `0.0..=1.0`; invalid/empty values are filtered at initialization; `default_snap_index` clamps to the last valid point.
`data-ars-placement` is emitted on both Positioner and Content parts because both layout wrappers and content CSS may need the resolved physical side.
Adapter specs (Leptos and Dioxus) must be updated alongside the core spec when core/adapter responsibility boundaries change â€” they had stale placement and velocity logic attributed to adapters.
`cargo xci --profile fast` is the mandatory pre-commit CI gate; commits require `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>` trailer.
New agnostic components require mutation testing with `cargo mutants` and llvm-cov; all missed mutants must be triaged in the same PR.
GitHub project board field IDs require GraphQL introspection (`gh api graphql`) to find the correct `optionId` for status transitions.
The proptest for state machine invariants must allow `DragEnd` to transition `Dragging â†’ Open` without emitting `OpenChange`, since logical open state doesn't change.

code.finding: Full Dialog implementation is the structural template: State/Event/Props/Context/Part/Effect enums, ConnectApi impl, inline #[cfg(test)] module with unit+snapshot tests.

New file. Velocity-based snap resolution must move from the *current* snap index in the movement direction, not from the nearest snap at the release offset â€” initial implementation had this wrong and was caught by a failing test.

Over-strict invariant: asserting OpenChange on every state-flipping transition is wrong for DragEnd (Draggingâ†’Open is not an open/close change). The check must be scoped to actual open/closed transitions only.

Spec had adapter-owned responsibilities for placement resolution and velocity/snap targeting; these belong to the agnostic core. DragEnd offset semantics also needed clarification: 0.0=fully open, 1.0=fully dismissed, positive velocity toward dismissal.

Added one justified equivalent-mutant skip for the negative velocity sign check inside `resolve_snap_index` â€” inside the `velocity.abs() > threshold` branch, `< 0.0` and `<= 0.0` cannot differ in behavior.

workflow: TDD sequence: write failing tests referencing the nonexistent module first, confirm compile failure, then implement until all pass â€” prevents accidentally green tests.
`cargo insta accept` must be run multiple times during snapshot test development as new snapshot test cases are added incrementally.
`cargo mutants` on a ~1900-line file takes ~29 minutes; run it once at the end rather than iterating on it, collecting the full missed list before patching.

`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` fails workspace-wide if any other unreferenced snapshots exist â€” use `--unreferenced=warn` with a `--glob-filter` for targeted per-component snapshot validation.
Plan Mode cannot be exited by the assistant itself; the user must paste the plan back as an explicit implementation instruction to enter Default mode.

Builder methods and public accessor helpers on Props/Context need direct mutation tests â€” proptest and behavioral unit tests alone will not catch all survivors because cargo-mutants will mutate the individual field assignments.

friction: `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` is unusable for per-component validation because pre-existing unrelated unreferenced snapshots elsewhere in the workspace cause the command to abort.

Velocity snap semantics were wrong in the initial implementation (snapping from nearest offset rather than from current snap in movement direction) â€” caught only by a failing unit test, not by spec reading.
Proptest invariant for `OpenChange` was over-strict; required a fix mid-run after proptest found a counterexample via `DragEnd`.
Plan Mode required user to explicitly paste the plan text as a new message to trigger implementation â€” the assistant could not exit Plan Mode on its own.
`cargo insta accept` needed multiple separate invocations as snapshot tests were added in stages, rather than one final accept pass.
GitHub project board status transition required multi-step GraphQL introspection (list project, list fields, find option ID) before a single `item-edit` mutation could be issued.
Mutation testing run took ~29 minutes, making the triage-and-rerun loop expensive (two full runs required).
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter Drawer components are not implemented â€” explicitly out of scope for #263 but will be separate follow-up issues.
Pre-existing unreferenced insta snapshots across the workspace need cleanup before `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` can be used safely as a CI gate.
Adapter specs (Leptos/Dioxus) were updated for responsibility boundaries but no adapter implementation exists yet to validate the spec corrections against real code.
